focus: Group live Cranfield failures by observed behavior, test candidate query architectures, and preserve measured accept/reject evidence.
reason_started: The operator asked to group failing Cranfield cases by behavior and try architecture changes one issue group at a time to improve evaluation techniques.
reason_ended: Failure behavior grouping is implemented, candidate architectures are opt-in, live comparison evidence is recorded, and field-sum is preserved as a transferability-pending candidate.

deployment.reason: GEN-011 is a local/live OpenSearch experiment; the public default remains ARCH-0.1 and no Worker deployment was requested.

validators_executed.name: npm run validate
validators_executed.evidence: 24 tests passed; MDE parser passed; traceability validator checked baseline and SE-0002 candidate IDs; fixture evaluation passed.

validators_executed.name: live baseline/query-rescue/field-sum evaluation
validators_executed.category: Evaluation Behavior Analysis / Architecture Experiment Comparison
validators_executed.evidence: All three live evaluations completed over 225 Cranfield queries with metrics at K=10 and retrieval depth 50.

validators_executed.name: npm run compare:cranfield
validators_executed.evidence: Comparison artifact classified query-rescue as regressed and field-sum as improved across core metrics.

validators_skipped.name: Cloudflare deploy verification
validators_skipped.reason: No deployment was requested and the candidate must not replace the public default before transferability evidence.

reusable_lessons: Aggregate search metrics should be paired with behavior-grouped query evidence before architecture changes are accepted.
A plausible query rescue strategy can regress metrics; rejected candidates should be preserved as evidence instead of deleted.
downstream_impacts: Phase 2 BEIR should include field-sum as the first transferability candidate.
Article 2 can explain why the next agent work should focus on specific failure groups instead of generic tuning.
content_seeds: Failure group chart for Article 2: 42 late-first-relevant, 28 zero-relevant, 27 lexical-noise, 25 broad-low-recall, 23 partial-recall, 16 graded-ranking-loss baseline cases.
Field-sum improved all core Cranfield metrics but still worsened 77 queries, making it a good example of evidence-based caution.
evidence_quality_notes: Live evaluation evidence is stronger than fixture evidence because it uses the full 225-query Cranfield qrel set and the configured OpenSearch index.
The candidate remains transferability-pending to avoid Cranfield overfitting.

supports_mission: Moves architecture through measured experiments rather than assumptions.
Improves the evaluation framework so readers can understand why metrics move.
Preserves accepted, rejected, and deferred experiment evidence.
missing: Public UI for browsing the new failure groups.
Deployment of GEN-010 versioned endpoint changes.
deferred: Promoting field-sum to a released architecture.
Deploying candidate architecture as a public default.
goal_drift_review.mission_alignment: GEN-011 directly supports evidence-driven search architecture evolution while keeping the public baseline stable.
